page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p-redis.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX-512 (including AVX-512 VNNI), AES, and FMA. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance sits primarily in the lower tiers for compute and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 25% of benchmarked hardware. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests show a cached read speed of 195.69 GB/sec and uncached read/write speeds of 25.91 GB/sec and 22.82 GB/sec respectively, both falling into the bottom 25%. While the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating, the PassMark database operations and static web serving throughput of 3.81 GB/sec remain in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable execution times compared to shared-core or burstable instances, though the overall hardware profile delivers modest performance per resource. Given the lack of local storage and the moderate memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is best utilized for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, or small-scale caching and database operations that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or high-throughput memory bandwidth.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p-redis.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng CPU tests, static web serving throughput, and uncached memory bandwidth all registering in the bottom 25%. However, the instance maintains an average PassMark memory score and supports AVX-512 instructions. This instance is configured for entry-level general-purpose workloads, lightweight web serving, and small-scale database operations where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over burstable resources but high-throughput performance is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p-redis.xlarge is a general-purpose KVM instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, showing modest entry-level benchmark performance.

tagline: A dedicated four-core Intel Xeon instance with sixteen gigabytes of memory for predictable, entry-level general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs with a 2.5 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with zero local storage
Supports AVX-512 and AES hardware-accelerated instructions
Suited for lightweight web serving and small-scale database tasks
